entry.summary: Valve just made some big changes to the sticker system, causing Counter-Strike 2 teams to lose a ton of revenue. This could spell trouble.

entry.summary: Snow-capped mountains carved by deep river valleys preside over northwestern Washington state.

entry.title: NVIDIA AI Supercomputer Comes Online at Naval Postgraduate School
entry.summary: NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined school leadership in Monterey, California, to commission the system, which will fuel AI education and research at the US military’s premier technical graduate university.

entry.title: FLCN Hadji ecstatic to be back in the international stage in MSC at EWC 2026: "I really miss this feeling."

entry.title: Exoplanet Travel Bureau: Proxima Centauri b - NASA Science
entry.summary: Tidally locked, ready to rock — and only 4.2 light-years from Earth

entry.title: Reusable magnetic invention removes microplastics plus some PFAS from water
entry.summary: Microplastics are an increasing global concern, with growing evidence of their presence in water systems. RMIT University researchers have developed a water treatment material that rapidly removes micro- and nanoplastics and some PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances), bringing the technology closer to real-world use. The invention builds on the team's 2022 breakthrough in microplastics removal, extending its performance to much smaller particles and more complex wastewater.

entry.title: Forget the burrito bowl: Karmine Corp's Blue Rising TCG is the future of esports monetization
entry.summary: Sick of the boring slapped on logos and lazy sponsored social media posts? Karmine Corp is showing how esports can truly thrive.

entry.title: Lich Queen Mei rises in new Overwatch x World of Warcraft collaboration!
entry.summary: The legendary Lich Queen Mei skin and Frostmourne Ana weapon have entered Overwatch as part of the game's latest World of Warcraft crossover.

entry.title: New optical method follows single proteins as they shift shape
entry.summary: Researchers at the University of Twente have developed an optical method that follows the shape changes of a single protein in liquid without attaching anything to it. It reads the protein's structure from its own molecular vibrations, free from the labels or tags that other techniques rely on. The method could help researchers study how proteins respond to drugs, toxins and other biomolecules. The paper is published in the journal ACS Nano.

entry.title: JWST Cycle 6 Call for Proposals - NASA Science
entry.summary: We are pleased to announce the Cycle 6 Call for Proposals for JWST General Observer (GO), Survey, and Archival Research (AR) programs. Proposals are due on 30 September 2026.

entry.title: Combined nanoparticle and internal laser method could make cancer therapy less invasive
entry.summary: In a promising development for cancer treatment, a collaborative research team has overcome two issues that have prevented photothermal therapy, a much less invasive treatment option than surgery and/or radiation, from becoming more common.

entry.title: FTC Returns Money to Consumers Harmed by Trend Deploy’s Deceptive Marketing
entry.summary: The Federal Trade Commission is sending more than $672,000 to consumers deceived by the operator of Trend Deploy, Frank Romero.

entry.summary: Medical device leaders are already building on NVIDIA’s new Medical Physics Simulation framework — giving surgical robots the virtual experience they need before interacting with patients.

This deepened commitment includes Microsoft’s new Scientific Partnership Advancing Research & Knowledge coordination hub and program office, otherwise...

entry.summary: A biomedical journal has retracted a paper by a cancer researcher who sued her former institution over misconduct allegations. The action comes a decade after a sleuth first raised concerns about t…

entry.summary: A new image of every point on the Earth’s landmass, captured once every 24 hours, with the ability to zoom in on specific locations at higher resolution.

entry.title: A Week of Smoky Skies Across North America - NASA Science
entry.summary: Brown carbon, emitted by wildland fires and tracked over the span of a week in July 2026, worsened air quality in parts of the U.S. and Canada.

entry.summary: League of Legends and Counter-Strike 2 seem to get all the love, but single-player games deserve way more attention.

entry.summary: We shared how Samsung users can boost productivity and get time back on new foldables, watches, and glasses coming soon.

entry.title: Ruthenium nanoparticles convert captured perchlorate into harmless chloride in water treatment waste
entry.summary: For decades, water utilities have relied on giant tanks filled with ion-exchange resin beads to remove perchlorate, a harmful industrial pollutant, from drinking water. The system works by attracting negatively charged perchlorate ions to positively charged resin beads, allowing purified water to flow out of the tanks. But the process leaves behind a difficult problem: resin beads loaded with perchlorate that must be regenerated or disposed of as hazardous waste.

entry.title: Built in Fort Worth: Wistron Opens Advanced Manufacturing Plant to Produce NVIDIA AI Systems
entry.summary: The AI era runs on AI infrastructure. Many of these advanced systems are built and tested in Texas. Wistron opened its first U.S. manufacturing facility today in Fort Worth — a 324,000-square-foot greenfield plant producing superchips at the heart of some of the world’s most capable AI systems. In front of an audience of Wistron […]

entry.title: I am not convinced that hunting for AI is going to solve esports' biggest issues

entry.title: What do people in middle-income countries think about cuts to U.S. humanitarian aid?
entry.summary: In 16 middle-income countries surveyed, many people are unsure how U.S. humanitarian aid cuts will affect them, though those with opinions lean negative.

entry.title: U.S. foreign aid is plunging in second Trump administration
entry.summary: U.S. foreign aid fell to its lowest inflation-adjusted level in over two decades in fiscal 2025, dropping to $47.3 billion — more than $24 billion less than the prior year.

entry.title: Overwatch reveals Shion's original face and Hashimoto elders in new story

entry.summary: NVIDIA and its partners are investing in American manufacturing, supply chains, energy grids and skilled workforces so the U.S. can produce the infrastructure needed for better healthcare, breakthrough scientific discovery, stronger industrial productivity and global technology leadership.

entry.title: Broad Institute, Boston Children’s Hospital, and The Jackson Laboratory launch the Center for Therapeutic Genetics, a non-profit effort to develop genetic medicines for rare and ultra-rare diseases
entry.summary: The new collaboration aims to develop precision medicines, including base and prime editing, to treat patients with rare diseases, using infrastructure and repeatable practices the center will share with others. div.hero-section__author { display:none;}

entry.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves has an insane DLC schedule, but it may not be enough to bring the sauce
entry.summary: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves devs are going ham with the DLC, dropping tons of characters. But do they have the sauce?

entry.title: Molecules stop carbon nanotubes clumping, unlocking record heat-to-electricity performance
entry.summary: QUT researchers have overcome a challenge that has limited next-generation energy-harvesting materials for more than two decades, opening the door to more powerful wearable electronics and new ways of turning wasted heat into electricity. The breakthrough centers on carbon nanotubes, which are flexible, conductive microscopic rods that have long shown promise for wearable technologies but have been difficult to control.

entry.title: Carbon nanotube model reveals how smart sensors perform at aircraft-like extremes
entry.summary: Researchers from Skoltech, in collaboration with the Harbin Institute of Technology and Jiangsu University (China), have published a study presenting the first empirical model to explain and predict the sensing behavior of hierarchical tri-phase carbon nanotube systems across a wide temperature range (from -170°C (-274°F) to 90°C (194°F)). The study has been published in the Carbon journal.
